question_title: Will PTC's market close price on 2026-04-22 be higher than its market close price on 2026-04-14?
summary: PTC is in a deep downtrend, trading ~39% below its 52-week high and near its 52-week low. The question asks whether the stock rebounds over 6 trading days (Apr 14→Apr 22). The empirical conditional base rate for PTC when in a 35%+ drawdown shows 54% positive 6-day returns (224 events), while the shock alert after the -4.1% single-day drop on Apr 10 shows only 45% positive outcomes. Company-specific headwinds (Barclays flagged negative checks, Kepware/ThingWorx divestiture removing ~$200M revenue, analyst downgrades) lean against recovery, while the broader S&P 500 recovery (+3.3% month) provides a modest tailwind. Monte Carlo simulations with zero drift give ~50%, while slight negative drift reflecting momentum and headwinds gives ~46%. Balancing the oversold mean-reversion tendency against persistent negative momentum and company-specific negatives, I estimate ~47% probability of a higher close on Apr 22.

factors.description: Empirical conditional base rate: when PTC is down >=35% from 52w high, 54% of 6-day forward returns are positive (224 events since 1989)
Strong negative recent momentum: -14.2% over 20 days, -7.1% over 5 days, persistent selling pressure
Near 52-week low ($130.89 intraday on Apr 13), only 2.2% above - potential capitulation/support zone
Company-specific headwinds: Barclays flagged 'mixed or negative checks', Kepware/ThingWorx divestiture removes ~$200M revenue, Mizuho downgraded to Neutral
Broader S&P 500 recovering (+3.3% over past month), positive market backdrop provides tailwind
Shock alert: after -4.1% single-day drop (Apr 10), empirical median forward return is -1.03% with only 45% positive over 10 days
Analyst consensus 'Moderate Buy' with average target $182 (36% upside), 95% institutional ownership
Earnings report Apr 29 (after resolution) - anticipation creates uncertainty but no clear directional bias

reasoning:
**Forecast: 47% probability** that PTC's close on Apr 22 will be higher than its Apr 14 close.

**Key reasoning:**
- PTC is deeply oversold (~39% drawdown from 52w high), and the empirical base rate for stocks in this condition shows 54% positive 6-day returns — slight mean-reversion
- However, company-specific negatives (Barclays flagged negative checks, $200M revenue removed via divestiture, multiple analyst downgrades) push against recovery
- The shock alert after the -4.1% drop on Apr 10 shows only 45% positive outcomes historically
- Strong negative momentum (-7% in 5 days, -14% in 20 days) suggests the trend hasn't reversed
- The broader S&P 500 recovery provides a modest tailwind but isn't enough to overcome the stock-specific headwinds

The net result is slightly below 50% — the stock is more likely to continue sliding or stay flat than to meaningfully bounce in the next 6 trading days.
sources_consulted: [PTC Inc. (PTC) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PTC/)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the latest market close price of PTC. If it is higher than the close price on 2026-04-14, the question will resolve to 'Yes'. The close price will be pulled from the Yahoo Finance API.
fine_print: Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
